title: justicia procumbens var. simplex
intro: Justicia procumbens var. simplex, also known as the creeping Justicia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Justicia procumbens var. simplex is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically to the skin for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
